title: add daily D1 to Tinybird sync for auth tables
author: ElliotEtag

gist.headline: Feeding the Tinybird
gist.blurb: We've set up a daily sync to securely pipe our auth data from D1 into Tinybird for better observability. Don't worry, all the sensitive bits stay safely locked in the vault!
gist.summary: Added a daily GitHub Actions cron job to securely sync non-sensitive authentication data from D1 to Tinybird for improved internal observability.
gist.impact: This gives the team better insights into platform usage and account metrics without exposing any sensitive user tokens or passwords.
gist.visual_concept: Polly the bee interacting with a literal 'tiny bird' to represent the Tinybird analytics sync. They are moving data (represented as glowing seeds or blocks) from a secure vault or retro server.
gist.image_prompt: Cozy pixel art scene of the cute bee mascot wearing a tiny hard hat, carefully handing glowing data-seeds to a tiny blue pixel bird perched on a retro server rack. Soft lime green (#ecf874) and lavender gradient background. Chunky pixels, warm lighting, lo-fi aesthetic like A Short Hike. Emotionally warm, nostalgic but beautiful.
